text:
10.9 That any co-operative society can manage 152 kinds of 
businesses apart from giving loan. Therefore, it is unfair to 
ignore all others important aspects which helps a society to 
run successfully.
10.10 That the minimum number of the membership for registration of 
a new society is 75 and the present appellant-society has given 
the list of 150 members. Therefore, the appellant-society is very 
much ahead of the minimum required number of memberships.
11. Per contra, the arguments advanced by the learned Senior Counsels 
and counsels for the respondents are stated as below:
11.1 That the eligibility for the purpose of registration cannot be 
isolated from the impact on an existing society, if any. The 
appellant herein has failed to satisfy the threshold criteria and 
conditions to establish its own viability. 
[2025] 5 S.C.R.  
419
Shri. Masaidevi Vividh Karyakari Sahakari Seva Sanstha 
Maryadit Warewadi v. The State of Maharashtra & Ors.
